question: Will real GDP increase by more than 2.0% in Q1 2026?
reasoning: The Atlanta Fed's GDPNow model has significantly deteriorated throughout Q1 2026, declining from over 3% in early March to just 1.3% as of April 7th. This nowcasting model incorporates real-time economic data and has strong predictive accuracy. The consistent downward trend, coupled with weak Q4 2025 GDP of only 0.7%, suggests momentum remains subdued heading into Q1 2026.
key_factors: Atlanta Fed GDPNow estimate at 1.3% (April 7) vs market threshold of 2.0%
Weak Q4 2025 GDP at 0.7% showing poor economic momentum
Consumer spending and private investment growth estimates deteriorating
GDPNow model has 1.17 percentage points RMSE accuracy
risks: BEA advance estimate could differ significantly from GDPNow nowcast
Remaining economic data releases could surprise positively
Model may not capture all economic dynamics affecting final GDP calculation
Government shutdown effects in Q4 may create statistical distortions
